_initial_state_note: Replaces the old num_incumbent_cohorts warm start (simulated cohorts admitted at negative terms). The university starts NOT empty: 'occupancy' is how many seats in each course are already taken by the existing student body the first simulated cohort competes against -- a steady-state background load subtracted from that course's free seats on every mandatory term (see src/simulator.py::_effective_capacity and CLAUDE.md's Initial-State Model). 'standing' is a rough head-count of existing students at each year-standing, added as a constant to the aggregate stage-flow chart so it starts non-empty. Edit these in Settings or the Scenario Builder. num_incumbent_cohorts still works as an engine knob (defaults to 0 / no incumbents) but is no longer part of the default plan.
_optional_terms_note: Fall and Spring are mandatory (the only seasons that advance a student's personal_semester clock / max_terms budget; see CLAUDE.md's Term/Season Model). Winter and Summer are optional intersessions: courses must explicitly list 'Winter'/'Summer' in their own offering array to run then, and use the separate, smaller optional_term_course_sections map (falling back to optional_term_capacity_scale x the regular section count) instead of course_sections. admit_interval_terms was raised from 2 to 4 to keep yearly, Fall-only admissions now that the cycle is 4 terms long instead of 2. Only cs_core courses are offered optionally (same 'squeeze CS, leave everyone else ample' philosophy as section_demand_percentile above) -- math/english/gen_ed courses are deliberately excluded: they're tier-2 (lowest) enrollment priority, so cs_core crowds them out of every regular term's load cap, building a multi-year backlog that would swamp a 1-section bonus offering (observed: ~1000+ capacity blocks on a single gen-ed course vs ~250 on the CS gateway) -- a real, if disproportionate, finding worth knowing about even though it's out of scope for this illustrative default. optional_terms_enabled defaults to FALSE: Winter/Summer are off (legacy 2-season Fall/Spring cycle) until an admin switches them on in Settings -> PUT /config. All the data above (terms_per_year, mandatory_terms, optional_term_course_sections, optional_term_capacity_scale, and the admit_interval_terms=4 yearly cadence) stays in this file inert the whole time; flipping the flag on re-activates it with no re-entry needed. admit_interval_terms auto-rescales to 2 while the flag is off, so Fall-only yearly admission is preserved either way -- see src/models/semester.py::effective_admit_interval_terms.
_sections_note: Per-term seats for a course = course_sections[code] x seats_per_section. CS courses (cs_core, cs_elective) are auto-calibrated by scripts/size_sections.py to the section_demand_percentile of per-term demand (0.75) so seats bind on CS gateways during enrolment bulges; non-CS courses are sized to full peak so they never bottleneck. Edit individual courses to add/cut sections. HAND-TUNE: CMPS303 (2->3) and CMPS350 (2->3) were raised one section above the demand-percentile baseline. Both were capacity-binding gateways in their mandatory terms (CMPS303 was the top capacity block; CMPS350 gates the CMPS493 compound rule). UPDATE (post optional-term model): this hand-tune now only lifts the 30-seed graduation rate by ~0.1pp (0.7329 -> 0.7342), an order of magnitude smaller than it was pre-optional-terms (0.708 -> 0.716), because Winter/Summer bonus sessions already absorb most of the retake demand a third mandatory section used to relieve. Both gateways are now fully unblocked in their mandatory terms (zero capacity denials) but their single-section optional-term bonus session is itself the most oversubscribed offering in the university -- see docs/project_overview.md for how the four block signals and the capacity model work. A 30-seed sweep of dropout_base_hazard (0.15-0.21) now moves graduation by 0.78pp, larger than this capacity sweep -- see _dropout_note.
_registration_tier_note: Seat-priority bands by completed_ch, checked in descending order (registration_tier 0 = registers first). QU's real priority-registration policy; a different institution redefines its own bands here, not in student.py::registration_tier.
_enrollment_priority_note: Phase-1 desired-enrollment order, tried after retakes (which always come first and are not part of this list). Each tier is {categories: [...], min_ch?: N}; a student's eligible courses are bucketed into the first tier whose categories contains the course's category AND whose min_ch (default 0) the student's completed_ch already meets, then tiers are filled in order up to the load cap. A category absent from every tier, or gated by an unmet min_ch, is simply not enrolled that term. This is QU CS's policy (required courses, then cs_electives once junior-standing CH is reached, then service/gen-ed courses) expressed as data; a different program redefines its own tiers/categories here, not in student.py.
_dropout_note: Dropout has two causes. PRIMARY (GPA): each term a student with completed_ch >= probation_min_ch and gpa < dropout_gpa_floor leaves with probability base_hazard*(1+(floor-gpa)), doubled (early_multiplier) in their first dropout_early_sem_cutoff semesters. SECONDARY (gateway): failing one course dropout_fails_threshold times drops them with prob dropout_prob_on_repeated_fail. CALIBRATION SCOPE: the only QU anchor (scripts/analyze_qu_data.py) is the AGGREGATE 72.3% 12-semester graduation rate — the open data is semester-level counts, not student-level histories, so dropout TIMING cannot be validated. The early_multiplier front-loading is a face-validity assumption, not a data fit. UPDATE (post optional-term model): a 30-seed sweep of base_hazard over 0.15-0.21 now moves graduation by 0.78pp (0.7382 -> 0.7304) with dropout/censored moving in opposite directions -- a real, monotone relationship, not the flat dropped<->censored trade found before optional terms existed. This swing is now LARGER than the gateway-capacity sweep's ~0.1pp (see _sections_note) -- the reverse of the original ranking. See docs/project_overview.md for how dropout/GPA mechanics work.
